reports.title: Market Pulse Daily
reports.subtitle: Weekend Edition (data as of 2026-03-25)
reports.stance: Constructive but cautious: okay to deploy selectively into quality large caps and strong structural themes, but avoid aggressive risk-taking in smallcaps until volatility and FII flows cool down.
reports.mainDriver: Global de-escalation hopes in the US–Israel–Iran conflict pulling down crude and supporting a two-day relief rally in Indian equities, even as the rupee stays weak and FIIs remain net sellers.
reports.summary: Nifty and Sensex staged a second strong relief rally on 25 March 2026 with broad-based gains led by PSU banks, infra and cyclicals, even as India VIX stayed elevated, FIIs continued to sell and the rupee hovered near record lows; the market regime is ‘volatile bullish’ with structural support from record public capex, defence indigenisation and manufacturing/semicon pushes, but also visible stress in weak smallcaps, governance-challenged microcaps and leveraged yield vehicles.

reports.summary: Stock markets rebounded as US-Iran tensions eased, but volatility is high. Focus remains on quality stocks and cautious positioning.

reports.title: Market Pulse Daily
reports.subtitle: Weekend Edition (data as of 2026-03-18)
reports.stance: Aggressive Capital Preservation and Defensive Posturing
reports.mainDriver: The Indian equity market is facing an unprecedented tri-factor shock this morning. First, Brent crude oil has violently surged past $112 per barrel following Iranian military strikes on the South Pars gas field and retaliatory missile launches toward Qatar and Saudi Arabia, threatening the critical Strait of Hormuz chokepoint. Second, the US Federal Reserve held interest rates at 3.50%-3.75% while adjusting its 'dot plot' to project only a single rate cut in 2026, officially embedding a 'higher for longer' cost of capital regime driven by sticky 2.7% PCE inflation. Third, and most damaging for domestic indices, HDFC Bank's part-time Chairman Atanu Chakraborty has abruptly resigned citing 'ethical concerns' and practices not in congruence with his personal values. This toxic combination of an exogenous energy shock, tight global liquidity, and a severe governance crisis at India's highest-weighted stock will trigger indiscriminate algorithmic and institutional liquidation at the opening bell.
reports.summary: March 19, 2026: A massive regime shift. GIFT Nifty indicates a 500+ point crash driven by Brent crude spiking to $112 after Middle East infrastructure attacks, a hawkish US Fed holding rates and projecting only one cut, and a severe governance shock at HDFC Bank. The report advises extreme defensive posturing, avoiding cyclicals, and hiding in IT and Coal India.

reports.title: Market Pulse Daily
reports.subtitle: Mean-reversion bounce
reports.mainDriver: Unprecedented domestic institutional liquidity absorbing fierce foreign capital flight amidst severe Middle East supply chain disruptions.
reports.summary: Comprehensive analysis of the Indian equity market amidst severe Middle East geopolitical escalation, Brent crude surging past $104, and extreme institutional polarization. Covers strategic Indian policy shifts (Semiconductors, PFC capital raise), severe small-cap valuation compression, governance red flags, and actionable swing trade/mutual fund deployment strategies.

reports.title: Market Pulse Daily
reports.subtitle: Range-bound uncertainty masking a structural risk-off correction in the broader market.
reports.mood: Range-bound uncertainty masking a structural risk-off correction in the broader market.
reports.regime: Range-bound uncertainty masking a structural risk-off correction in the broader market.
reports.stance: Defensive Capital Preservation with Selective Accumulation
reports.mainDriver: The intersection of extreme Middle East geopolitical instability (Strait of Hormuz disruption) and relentless foreign capital outflows, offset only by aggressive domestic institutional buying in large-cap blue-chips.
reports.summary: March 16, 2026: The Indian equity market staged a deceptive 900-point relief rally on the Sensex, driven by short-covering as Brent crude temporarily cooled following a historic IEA reserve release. However, internal market breadth collapsed, FIIs dumped ₹10,716 Cr, and the VIX surged above 22, signaling a highly toxic, risk-off environment. The IDBI Bank divestment collapsed, shattering the PSU privatization narrative. Strategic focus must shift exclusively to domestic power infrastructure, telecom hardware, and capital preservation.

reports.title: Market Pulse Daily
reports.subtitle: Panic sell-off
reports.mainDriver: Escalating US-Iran conflict and crude oil surging above $100 triggered a massive 1,470-point Sensex crash on Friday, with severe FII dumping overwhelming the market before the weekend.
reports.summary: Deep-dive daily market research report for the weekend of March 14-15, 2026. Analyzes the massive Friday market crash driven by the escalating US-Iran conflict, Brent crude sustaining above $100, and severe FII dumping. Highlights the stealth correction in broader markets, the flight to safety in FMCG, and the high-risk environment demanding capital preservation.

reports.stance: Defensive bias; reduce risk, keep dry powder, deploy only in small tranches into relative strength
reports.mainDriver: Middle East war + crude above $100 -> rupee at record low + persistent FII de-risking -> broad Indian risk-off sell-off.
reports.summary: Risk-off correction strengthened: Nifty -2.06%, VIX ~22.75, rupee at record low close 92.46/$; sector rotation favors defensives while metals/auto/PSU banks lead the fall.

reports.title: Market Pulse Daily
reports.subtitle: Risk-off correction
reports.stance: Defensive and patient: protect capital first, add only on strength or after stabilization.
reports.mainDriver: Iran-war driven crude spike + heavy FII selling + rupee making new lows => repricing of risk.
reports.summary: Risk-off correction day: Nifty -2.06% to 23,151; VIX >22; crude >$100; rupee at record low ~92.46; FIIs sold ~₹10.7k Cr while DIIs bought ~₹10.0k Cr; cyclicals (metals/auto/PSU banks) hit hardest; FMCG was lone shelter.

reports.stance: Defensive first, selective offense only in relative-strength pockets.
